Detail: Secure desktop window with process isolation and no direct filesystem access from the renderer
Native OS dialog to select a project directory
Scan directory for files matching pattern NN-name.ext where ext is md/csv/dbml
Generate tabs sorted by numeric prefix with extension color indicators
Route each tab to the correct editor component based on file extension
Read and write project files through a secure inter-process boundary
Rich text editing with markdown syntax highlighting
Side-by-side editor (left) and rendered HTML preview (right)
Draggable divider between editor and preview panels
Display CSV data in an editable grid with column headers from first row
Click-to-edit cells with value persistence
Cells starting with = are evaluated as spreadsheet formulas
Parse and serialize CSV preserving quoting and special characters
Syntax highlighting for DBML keywords; types; strings; and comments
Real-time entity-relationship diagram rendered from DBML source
Zoom in/out/reset controls for the SVG preview panel
Show last valid SVG on parse error with error message overlay
Visual indicator (*) on tabs with unsaved changes
Save active editor content to disk via keyboard shortcut
Detect external file changes via chokidar and reload clean buffers
Application's own file writes do not trigger an external-change reload
Ctrl+Tab and Ctrl+Shift+Tab to cycle through tabs
Click tab to switch active editor
Code editor loads entirely from local assets with no network requests
Dark neutral color scheme for IDE-like appearance
Landing screen with project directory picker when no project is open
Remember and display recently opened project directories
Insert and remove rows from the spreadsheet grid
Insert and remove columns with header editing
Click column header to sort rows ascending/descending
Toolbar buttons for bold/italic/heading/link/list formatting
Optionally auto-save after a configurable debounce period
Click-and-drag panning of the SVG diagram
Export rendered markdown or DBML diagrams to PDF
Frameless window with custom minimize/maximize/close controls
